name: Jerry Lewis
description: American comedian, actor and film director (1926–2017)
abstract: Jerry Lewis (born Jerome Levitch but often given as Joseph Levitch; March 16, 1926 – August 20, 2017) was an American comedian, actor, singer, filmmaker and humanitarian. Across his seven-decade career, Lewis was regarded as one of the greatest comedians of the 20th century.

concerts.label: The New York Pops 12th Birthday Gala Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Rivello
about_quotes.evidence: And lots of people know that because it was in the Jerry Lewis movie, you know, “The Nutty Professor” or “The Absent-Minded Professor,” whichever one it was with the black and white.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Rivello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Sex and the Single Girl was another movie we did with Jerry Lewis
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Dean Martin, Jerry Lewis; all the comedians – Don Rickles, Red Foxx, Fatjack Leonard; all the singers – Peggy Lee, Pearl Bailey, Lena Horne. Everybody in show business loved us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: In the old days it wasn’t like a Jerry Lewis Telethon where they rehearse all week and they already had 80 million dollars that the corporations gave them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: So we were doing a film, “Funny Bones” which is a wonderful comedy with Jerry Lewis... And I had written basically an orchestral score
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Because at that time, Jerry Lewis was calling and all these kind of guys. He says, “No, we’re going to do . . . .” Jerry was good.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
